Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple An expert-level threat actor group named Versa Director conducts a zero-day exploitation campaign targeting internet-facing assets of transportation systems. They aim to disrupt operations through data destruction.
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Command and Scripting Interpreter, File Modification

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-28204 (Suggested): Sensitive information disclosure via out-of-bounds read in WebKit
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121; Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces
stage.description: An attacker exploits a zero-day vulnerability in Versa Director servers to gain initial access to the network, bypassing authentication mechanisms.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2025-34028 (Suggested): Arbitrary code execution via path traversal in Commvault Command Center
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 160; Name: Exploit Script-Based APIs
stage.description: Following initial access, the attacker executes malicious scripts using command-line interfaces to gain further control over compromised systems.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 675; Name: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker proceeds to delete critical files, causing disruption to transportation system operations.

Scenario.summary: An expert-level threat actor group exploits a zero-day vulnerability in Versa Director servers to gain unauthorized access to a transportation system's network. After gaining entry, they execute malicious scripts to escalate their privileges and ultimately cause data destruction, disrupting the targeted services.
